page: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip and bzip3 single-threaded benchmarks reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. Multi-threaded performance is solid, with stress-ng multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth write operations scoring in the average range. The processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 capabilities contribute to strong performance in specialized workloads, as evidenced by top-tier text generation speeds during small-model LLM inference.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D48lds_v7 is its low memory density relative to its compute capacity. With only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, it is less suitable for memory-intensive applications but offers high compute density. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of a large 2835 GB local disk provides substantial local storage capacity without requiring separate storage attachments, optimizing the resource-to-cost ratio for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local scratch space.

This server configuration is suited for compute-bound workloads, web serving, single-threaded data processing, and database operations that benefit from fast local storage and strong single-core CPU execution. It is also applicable for lightweight LLM inference tasks and cryptography-heavy applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 2835 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, with single-threaded gzip and bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier levels. The processor supports Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, which drive strong performance in small-model LLM inference and cryptographic tasks. While multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average, the server's high compute density and large bundled local storage make it highly efficient for compute-bound applications, static web serving, and database operations requiring fast local scratch space.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance and top-tier decompression speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v7 features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-dense platform combining high-frequency Intel Xeon processing with substantial local storage for demanding single-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM inference performance
Strong database operations and static web serving throughput
Suited for compute-bound applications, cryptography, and local-storage-heavy workloads
